title: Protodyakonov strength index
aim: To determine the Protodyakonov strength index of rock .
theory: Protodyakonov Strength Index test is used to determine the hardness or strength of rock.
It gives a strength index (f value) which represents the resistance of rock to crushing.
The test is based on the impact (dynamic loading) principle.
A rock sample is subjected to repeated hammer blows to break it into smaller pieces.
The degree of crushing indicates the strength of the rock.
After crushing, the material is sieved to separate fine particles.
The amount of fines produced is used to calculate the strength index.
Stronger rocks produce fewer fines, while weaker rocks produce more fines.
The Protodyakonov index (f) is inversely related to the degree of crushing.
It is widely used in mining and tunneling for rock classification.
The test is simple, quick, and suitable for field conditions.
The index helps in selecting excavation methods and equipment.
procedure: The given rock sample is first broken into small, uniform-sized pieces as specified in the experiment, removing weathered or weak portions.
The prepared sample is cleaned and dried to eliminate moisture and dust for accurate results.
A known weight of the sample (as prescribed in the lab manual) is taken for the test.
The weighed sample is placed inside a standard cylindrical steel mortar (test cylinder).
A standard hammer or weight is positioned above the sample and allowed to fall freely from a fixed height.
The sample is subjected to a specified number of blows to ensure uniform application of impact energy.
Due to repeated impacts, the rock sample is crushed into smaller fragments and fine particles.
After the required number of blows, the crushed material is carefully removed from the cylinder.
The material is sieved using a standard sieve as specified.
The weight or quantity of fine particles passing through the sieve is measured accurately.
Using this value, the Protodyakonov Strength Index (f) is calculated as per the given formula.
The experiment is repeated for multiple trials, and the average strength index is reported as the final result.
result: The PSI is 333kgf/cm^2 and 180kgf/cm^2 for sample 1 and Sample 2 respectively.
